Objective to provide morphological basis for free gracilis muscle transplantation to manage brachial plexus injuries.
Conclusion The semitendinosus and gracilis tendon could regenerate after harvested for anterior cruciate ligament reconstruction.
The specimens were determined to range from tiny lizards such as O. gracilis, to snakes as large as a modern boa constrictor, to meat-eating lizards up to 2m (6ft 6in) long.
